rationale: The page provides detailed information on cotton and opium cultivation in India, including climatic requirements, production statistics, and legal frameworks like the NDPS Act. It also introduces the BHARATI initiative by APEDA.
cleanedText:
6.7.3. COTTON
Why in the News?
In view of the persistent demand–supply gap, the Government has extended the import duty exemption on cotton until 31st December 2025.
Cotton
• It is also termed as “White-Gold”.
• Climatic Conditions:
o Cotton is a natural fiber and a semi-xerophyte tropical crop grown in the kharif season in semi-arid regions of India.
o Cotton is grown on a variety of soils ranging from well drained deep alluvial soils (North) to black clayey soils (Central) and in black red soils (South) region in India.
o It is semi-tolerant to salinity and sensitive to water logging.
o Requires high temperature, light rainfall and ~210 frost-free days and bright sunshine for its growth.
• Production in India
o India is the second largest producer of cotton after China.
o India is the only country which grows all four species of cotton.
o Major States: Maharashtra, Gujarat, Telangana, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh.
6.7.4. OPIUM CULTIVATION
Why in the News?
Union Government has announced Annual Licensing Policy for Opium Cultivation for 2025-26 under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Rules, 1985, framed under the NDPS Act, 1985.
About Opium
• Opium poppy plant is the source of opium gum which contains several indispensable alkaloids (naturally occurring organic nitrogen-containing compounds) such as morphine, codeine and thebaine.
o Morphine is commonly used analgesic (pain relieving medicine) while codeine is used in manufacture of cough syrups.
• It is also grown as a source of edible seed and seed oil.
• India is the only country authorised by the United Nations Single Convention on Narcotic Drugs (1961) to produce gum opium.
o 11 other countries cultivate opium poppy, but do not extract gum.
Opium Cultivation in India
• NDPS Act allows the Central Government to regulate opium poppy cultivation for medical and scientific use.
• Government annually notifies licensed tracts, currently in Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh.
• Central Bureau of Narcotics (CBN) issues licenses; cultivators must sell all opium to CBN at government-set prices.
6.8. OTHER KEY DEVELOPMENTS
6.8.1. BHARATI INITIATIVE
APEDA has launched BHARATI to support Agri Food Startups and boost India’s agri Food Export.
